content.full_text:
SACRAMENTO ADDRESS
State Capitol
P.O. Box 942849
Sacramento, CA 94249-0001
TELEPHONE: (916) 445-2931
FAX: (916) 445-3832

DISTRICT OFFICE
2929 West Main Street
Suite "J"
Visalia, CA 93291
TELEPHONE: (209) 734-1182
FAX: (209) 733-6478

DISTRICT OFFICE
2497 W. Shaw, Suite 106
Fresno, CA 93711
TELEPHONE: (209) 224-7833
FAX: (209) 224-7835

FROM: Porterville, Dinuba, Exeter,
Three Rivers and Springville

ENTERPRISE: 18463

FPPC

SEP 17 1991

Assembly
California Legislature

BILL JONES
ASSEMBLYMAN, THIRTY-SECOND DISTRICT
ASSEMBLY REPUBLICAN LEADER

September 11, 1991

Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request For Advice on Use of Campaign Funds

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This request for written advice, pursuant to section
83114(b), is submitted on behalf of Assembly Minority Leader Bill
Jones. I am the chairman of Mr. Jones's controlled committees,
and he has authorized me to make this request on his behalf.

Assemblyman Jones has an "officeholder" committee, the Bill
Jones Republican Leadership Fund, and a "re-elect" committee,
Bill Jones for Assembly 1992. Each committee has a campaign bank
account into which campaign funds are deposited. Bill Jones
holds his position as Assembly Minority Leader because he was
elected to serve by a majority of Assembly Republicans.
Assemblyman Jones has filed a statement of intention to run for
re-election to the Assembly in 1992.

As Assembly Minority Leader, Assemblyman Jones has
political, legislative and governmental responsibilities in
addition to representing his constituents. For example, he is
expected to provide governmental and legislative leadership for
members of his Caucus, which involves frequent meetings with them
on legislative matters. He is also responsible for helping to
elect Republican members, to increase the numbers in his Caucus
and thus enhance their ability to be effective in the legislative
process. He is expected to support Republican candidates
generally and to play an active role in candidate recruitment.

In his capacity as Minority Leader, Assemblyman Jones will
frequently participate in meetings with active as well as
potential Republican candidates. Often, these meetings involve
civic as well as political leaders. The meetings are commonly
held in the area where the candidates live, which frequently is
not within easy driving distance of either Fresno or Sacramento.

NOT PRINTED AT GOVERNMENT EXPENSE

Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel
September 11, 1991
Page 2


Assemblyman Jones wishes to make expenditures from his
"officeholder" or "re-elect" accounts which relate to his duties
as Minority Leader, including contributions to other candidates
and payments associated with candidate recruitment. He assumes
initially that transfers from one of his campaign bank accounts
to the other may be pursued pursuant to the decision and order of
the federal district court in Service Employees International
Union v. Fair Political Practices Commission, 747 F.Supp. 580
(B.D. Cal. 1990), and staff advice based on SEIU (e.g., Maravitch
Advice Letter, I-91-199.)¹ Assemblyman Jones is aware that in
light of SEIU, the Commission has advised that a candidate may
transfer (contribute) funds to another candidate (Dorman Advice
Letter (I-90-253) if the transfer bears a reasonable relationship
to a political purpose.

Assemblyman Jones would prefer to make contributions to
other candidates from his "officeholder" account, and preserve his
"re-elect" account for traditional re-election expenditures,
consistent with the expectations of many of his contributors. He
believes that in view of his position as Minority Leader,
contributions from his campaign funds to other Republican
candidates also bear a reasonable relationship to a governmental
or legislative purpose. "Officeholder" expenditures to defray
the reasonable travel expenses of meeting with actual and
potential candidates would also be helpful to him in discharging
his legislative responsibility as Republican Minority Leader.²

To ensure that he meets the legal requirements for use of
campaign funds, Assemblyman Jones requests the Commission's
advice on application of Chapter 9.5, Article 4 (Campaign Funds)
of the Political Reform Act to the following proposed
expenditures:

1. In view of his position as Minority Leader, may
Assemblyman Jones make contributions from his campaign funds to

_____________________

1 I understand that the SEIU case is on appeal to the Ninth
Circuit and a ruling is expected shortly. Assemblyman Jones
would appreciate a response to his questions based on the status
of the SEIU case at the time of your response. However, he
requests that you not delay your response in anticipation of the
court ruling.

2 See Government Code section 89513(a)(1): "For purposes
of this section, payments or reimbursements for travel and
necessary accommodations shall be considered as directly related
to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose if the
payments would meet standards similar to the standards of the
Internal Revenue Service pursuant to sections 162 and 274 of the
Internal Revenue Code...."

Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel
September 11, 1991
Page 3


Republican candidates for Assembly, regardless of the particular
assembly district jurisdiction in which the candidates are
running? May he contribute to other Republican candidates, such
as candidates for statewide or local office, from campaign
funds? If contributions to such candidates are permissible,
may the contributions be made "in-kind"--that is, by payment
directly to vendors for polling, mailings and similar
expenditures?

2. Assuming making monetary or in-kind contributions to
Assembly and/or to other Republican candidates is a permitted use
of Bill Jones' campaign funds, may Assemblyman Jones make the
contributions from either his "officeholder" or "re-elect"
accounts, or must the contributions be made from one or the
other? If a contribution must be made from a particular account,
from which account must it be made?

3. May Assemblyman Jones use campaign funds to pay ordinary
travel expenses (including reasonable meals and accommodations)
for meetings with potential Republican Assembly candidates? For
meetings with other potential Republican candidates? If so, may
such expenditures be made from either his "officeholder" or his
"re-elect" committee campaign bank accounts? If not, from which
account should such expenditures be made?

Assemblyman Jones would appreciate a response to this
request for advice at your earliest convenience. Please notify
our counsel, Charles H. Bell, Jr. or Judith Sproul Davis at
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson, Parrinello & Mueller if a response
cannot be provided within 21 working days.

Thank you for your consideration.

Very truly yours,

Bob Jennings, Chairman
Bill Jones for Assembly 1992
Bill Jones Republican Leadership Fund

BJ/kab

cc: Assemblyman Bill Jones
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson, Parrinello & Mueller

3 If a candidate is running in a special election,
Assemblyman Jones understands that he may not contribute to that
candidate under the SEIU ruling as interpreted by the FPPC staff.
(Gov. Code § 85305.)
